zh.lifeSummary: Chevalier Pierre Guillaume II De Kaër Seigneur de Plessis Kaer , de Keriguer是Chevalier Olivier I DE KAER Seigneur de Plessis Kaer的兒子，於1180年出生在法國布列塔尼莫比韓洛可馬利亞可爾。他和dame Plézou De Kerambourg至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .

Indian (Maharashtra): probably a nickname from Marathi kir ‘parrot’.

variants.name: Kear
meanings: English (Gloucestershire): occupational name for a locksmith, from Middle English keyer, kayer ‘key-maker, locksmith’ (Old English cǣgere, a derivative of cǣg ‘key’).

nameMeanings.name: Pierre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From New Testament Greek. See Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Germanic. See William .

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: Chevalier Pierre Guillaume II De Kaër Seigneur de Plessis Kaer , de Keriguer was born in 1180, in Locmariaquer, Morbihan, Brittany, France as the son of Chevalier Olivier I DE KAER Seigneur de Plessis Kaer. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with dame Plézou De Kerambourg.

fr.lifeSummary: Chevalier Pierre Guillaume II De Kaër Seigneur de Plessis Kaer , de Keriguer est né en 1180, à Locmariaquer, Morbihan, Bretagne, France, fils de Chevalier Olivier I DE KAER Seigneur de Plessis Kaer. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec dame Plézou De Kerambourg.

de.lifeSummary: Chevalier Pierre Guillaume II De Kaër Seigneur de Plessis Kaer , de Keriguer wurde 1180 in Locmariaquer, Morbihan, Bretagne, Frankreich, als Sohn von Chevalier Olivier I DE KAER Seigneur de Plessis Kaer geboren. Er hatte mit dame Plézou De Kerambourg mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Chevalier Pierre Guillaume II De Kaër Seigneur de Plessis Kaer , de Keriguer è nato il nel 1180 nel Locmariaquer, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia come figlio di Chevalier Olivier I DE KAER Seigneur de Plessis Kaer. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con dame Plézou De Kerambourg.

ja.lifeSummary: Chevalier Pierre Guillaume II De Kaër Seigneur de Plessis Kaer , de Keriguerは1180年にフランスブレタグンモービハンロクマリアクエルでChevalier Olivier I DE KAER Seigneur de Plessis Kaerの息子として生まれた。彼はdame Plézou De Kerambourgとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Chevalier Pierre Guillaume II De Kaër Seigneur de Plessis Kaer , de Keriguer(은)는 1180년 프랑스 브레타그니 모르비핸 록마이아크어에서 Chevalier Olivier I DE KAER Seigneur de Plessis Kaer의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 dame Plézou De Kerambourg(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Chevalier Pierre Guillaume II De Kaër Seigneur de Plessis Kaer , de Keriguer nasceu em 1180, em Locmariaquer, Morbihan, Bretagne, França filho de Chevalier Olivier I DE KAER Seigneur de Plessis Kaer. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com dame Plézou De Kerambourg.

ru.lifeSummary: Chevalier Pierre Guillaume II De Kaër Seigneur de Plessis Kaer , de Keriguer родился в 1180 г. в местности локмарякуир, морбихань, бретагн, Франция. Его отца звали Chevalier Olivier I DE KAER Seigneur de Plessis Kaer. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от dame Plézou De Kerambourg.

es.lifeSummary: Chevalier Pierre Guillaume II De Kaër Seigneur de Plessis Kaer , de Keriguer nació en 1180, en Locmariaquer, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia como hijo de Chevalier Olivier I DE KAER Seigneur de Plessis Kaer. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija con dame Plézou De Kerambourg.
